title: Activation Energy

content.question: What setup step can you eliminate that would make a habit near-automatic?
sop: Pick one habit you keep intending to do but consistently skip.
List every setup step required before the actual habit can start.
Rate the Activation Energy 1-10 of the current setup (see calculation, page 5)
Eliminate or pre-do the highest-friction setup step (lay out clothes, prep ingredients, open the doc).
Re-rate Activation Energy after the change and confirm Start Probability actually went up (see calculation, page 5)
questions: What setup step can you eliminate that would make a habit near-automatic?
What do you have to do before you can even begin the habit you keep skipping?
If the habit took zero setup, would you still be skipping it, or is setup the actual problem?

definitions.def: The minimum effort required to start a process, in habits, the friction standing between you and beginning.

content.equation_example: Rate Activation Energy 1-10 (how much setup or friction exists before you can start). Activation Energy 8, Start Probability equals 1 divided by 8 equals 0.125 (12.5%). Activation Energy 2, Start Probability equals 1 divided by 2 equals 0.5 (50%). Higher activation energy means a lower chance you'll actually start. Lower activation energy means starting becomes nearly automatic.
content.closing_statement: Most people lack a low-friction start, not motivation.

caption:
Principle Nº 159: Activation Energy

Lower start resistance.

Ask yourself: What setup step can you eliminate that would make a habit near-automatic?
